summary: Ported the LinkLan design handoff into the Tauri+React app on feat/linklan-ui-full-redesign. Replaced index.css tokens end-to-end (colors, spacing, radii, shadows, Plus Jakarta + JetBrains Mono, every keyframe from README). Rewrote App.tsx into a 3-column shell (320 sidebar / flex-1 chat / 320 detail) under a 26px rounded card with a 36px macOS-style title bar. Built a Sidebar (Brand, Search, Tabs, ConvoRow, DeviceList with pulse-ring + rescan, SelfRow backed by new get_device_info Rust command), a Chat (ChatHeader, MessageList with IME-safe autoscroll, TextBubble with tail radii 18/18/18/6 vs 18/18/6/18, FileBubble wired to the transfers store with shimmer progress + cancel, Composer with IME composition guard covering onCompositionStart/End + nativeEvent.isComposing + keyCode 229), a Detail panel (PeerHero, QuickActions with pin toggle, Tabs, FileList, InfoList), and Overlays (DropOverlay wired to Tauri v2 onDragDropEvent, Toast + ToastStack). Added a new ui.ts Zustand store for activeConvoId / sidebarTab / detailOpen / dragOver / pinnedIds / toasts with localStorage persistence (ll-active / ll-detail-open / ll-pinned). On the Rust side added get_device_info returning { id, name, hostname, ip, os } with a UDP-connect best-effort local IP, and fixed a pre-existing socket2 feature gap (features = ["all"]) blocking cargo check. Added 10 new Vitest component tests (TextBubble tail radii + fresh-pop class, ConvoRow unread capsule + 99+ clamp, Composer Enter/Shift+Enter + IME composition guard + keyCode 229 fallback) on top of the 23 existing store tests — all 33 pass. Lint, test, cargo check, and vite build are all clean.

tests.vite-build: built in <1s (330 kB JS / 20.6 kB CSS)

followups: Backend PeerInfo does not yet carry an OS marker, so DeviceRow renders the generic '设备' badge for most peers — wire once protocol supports an `os` field in the heartbeat packet.

FileBubble/Detail FileList show all transfers when the backend hasn't yet populated FileTransfer.peer_id/direction; add a Rust-side mapping so files filter correctly per-peer and display the correct arrow direction.
Attach button currently shows a toast pointing to drag-drop; swap for `tauri-plugin-dialog` open() once the plugin is wired.
TypingIndicator was designed in Phase 4 but removed from this port because the backend does not yet emit a typing event — add back along with a `typing-ping` Rust event.
Legacy `useAppStore.activeTab` and `contactsStore.select/selectedId` are retained only for the existing 23 store tests; consider cleaning up in a follow-up once tests are updated.
